latest.name: Claude Opus 4.8
latest.headline: Anthropic's most capable model yet — better coding, reasoning, and honesty.
highlights: Agentic coding score up from 64.3% to 69.2%
~4× less likely to let a code flaw pass without comment
Fast mode runs at 2.5× speed and 3× cheaper than before
Dynamic workflows: hundreds of parallel subagents in Claude Code

timeline.title: Introducing Claude for Small Business
timeline.summary: We're launching Claude for Small Business, a package of connectors and ready-to-run workflows that put Claude inside the tools small businesses use every day.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Haiku 4.5
timeline.summary: Claude Haiku 4.5, our latest small model, is available today to all users.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.5
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Claude Opus 4.6
timeline.summary: We’re upgrading our smartest model. Across agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance, Opus 4.6 is an industry-leading model, often by wide margin.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
timeline.summary: Our latest model, Claude Opus 4.7, is now generally available. Opus 4.7 is a notable improvement on Opus 4.6 in advanced software engineering, with particular gains on the most difficult tasks.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
timeline.summary: Our latest model, Claude Opus 4.8, is an upgrade to our Opus class of models, with stronger performance across coding, agentic tasks, and professional work, and the consistency to handle long-running work.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.5
timeline.summary: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is the best coding model in the world, strongest model for building complex agents, and best model at using computers.

timeline.title: Skills
timeline.summary: New from Anthropic — see the announcement for details.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5
timeline.summary: Our most agentic Sonnet yet, with top-tier intelligence for coding and everyday professional work.

timeline.title: Introducing Sonnet 4.6
timeline.summary: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is a full upgrade of the model’s skills across coding, computer use, long-reasoning, agent planning, knowledge work, and design.

timeline.title: Apply for Anthropic’s AI for Science rare disease research grants
timeline.summary: Anthropic is sharing a focused call for AI for Science applications centered specifically on rare genetic diseases. Accepted applicants will receive up to $50,000 in Claude credits over six months, with the goal of building a community of researchers looking into how AI can reshape our understanding of rare disease.

timeline.title: Agents for financial services
timeline.summary: We're releasing ten new Cowork and Claude Code plugins, integrations with the Microsoft 365 suite, new connectors, and an MCP app for financial services and insurance organizations.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Tag
timeline.summary: Claude Tag is a new way for teams to work with Claude.

timeline.title: Anthropic commits $10 million to Canadian AI research
timeline.summary: Anthropic is committing $10M to Canadian research institutions to fund the next generation of AI research.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude for Teachers
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Anthropic Sydney office
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Claude for Creative Work
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
timeline.summary: Today, we’re launching Claude Design, a new Anthropic Labs product that lets you collaborate with Claude to create polished visual work like designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers, and more.

timeline.title: UST is bringing Claude to physical AI
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: The Long-Term Benefit Trust
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

upcoming.title: Claude Opus 4.9 / 5.0
upcoming.summary: Expected next flagship step. Watch for larger effort-control range and deeper agent orchestration.

upcoming.title: Expanded dynamic workflows
upcoming.summary: Parallel subagent orchestration expanding beyond Enterprise/Team/Max in Claude Code.

upcoming.title: Longer context tier
upcoming.summary: Competitive pressure from 2M-token rivals; a larger context option is plausible.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.note: Best-in-class agentic coding and the most reliable at flagging its own mistakes. The reference point for this comparison.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.note: Strongest all-rounder; broad ecosystem and tooling. Slightly pricier on input than Claude.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Leads published reasoning benchmarks and is the fastest of the group. Cheapest output among the frontier tier.

models.name: Grok 4
models.note: Largest context window at 2M tokens; strong raw coding scores.

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Pro
models.note: By far the cheapest frontier option — open-weight MoE, ~1.6T params. Great cost-to-performance.

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.note: Open weights, self-hostable. Lower benchmark scores but unbeatable on price and control.

models.name: Mistral Large 3
models.note: European, strong on efficiency and multilingual tasks. Smaller context window than rivals.

samples.text: Opus 4.8 is genuinely the best coding model I've used — caught a race condition Cursor missed.
samples.label: positive

samples.text: Love that 4.8 flags its own uncertainty now instead of bluffing. Huge for trust.
samples.label: positive

samples.text: Fast mode on 4.8 still feels slow for me and the pricing stings vs Gemini.
samples.label: negative

samples.text: Solid bump but honestly nothing mind-blowing over 4.7 for my workflow.
samples.label: neutral

benchmarks.note: SWE-bench Pro (coding) and a blended reasoning index (GPQA/ARC-AGI). Figures are approximate, sourced from public leaderboards; see Sources. Lower is better for price.

metrics.label: Reasoning index
metrics.desc: Blended GPQA Diamond / ARC-AGI style reasoning score.

metrics.label: Output speed
metrics.desc: Generation throughput in tokens per second.

metrics.label: Output price
metrics.desc: Cost per million output tokens (lower is better).
